skipped.heading: multi_dispatch 兼容

skipped.heading: 后果
skipped.reason: Outcome/risk summary rather than focused mechanism

drafts.title: Dispatch exposes primitives, not strategies
drafts.compiledTruth: Dispatch should expose small delegation primitives: one task via dispatch_agent and parallel fanout via dispatch_parallel/dispatch_agents. The main session owns orchestration decisions in-context: whether to run another round, synthesize with a different model, chain outputs, or add reviewers dynamically. Fixed strategies should not be the control surface because they force lossy mapping from real task intent to canned modes.

drafts.title: Strategies are cookbook patterns
drafts.compiledTruth: Parallel, debate, chain, and ensemble are reusable orchestration patterns, not API enum values. They belong in prompt guidance, snippets, or skills that teach the main LLM when and how to compose dispatch calls. The LLM may start from these recipes but can stop rounds, switch models, add observers, or mix patterns based on intermediate results.
drafts.sourceHeading: 现有四种 strategy 的处置

drafts.title: Compat belongs in argument prep
drafts.compiledTruth: Registered dispatch tools keep strict parameter schemas; input leniency lives in the argument-preparation hook. That hook normalizes accidental JSON-stringified arrays/objects before schema validation, using shared dispatch input-compat code. Internal typed APIs do not use this compatibility path because code-to-code calls should pass typed values directly.
drafts.sourceHeading: 输入兼容契约(兼容 JSON 字符串与对象输入)

drafts.title: Do not hide compat with Any
drafts.compiledTruth: Do not weaken dispatch tool schemas with Type.Any or equivalent just to accept malformed model/RPC arguments. The contract remains strict at the registered tool boundary; compatibility is an explicit preprocessing layer. This preserves clear validation, focused normalization, and useful model-facing errors instead of making every downstream consumer handle ambiguous shapes.
drafts.sourceHeading: 输入兼容契约(兼容 JSON 字符串与对象输入)

drafts.title: Compat is field-specific
drafts.compiledTruth: Dispatch input compatibility is opt-in per known field. tasks/options may unwrap stringified arrays or objects, task.tools may convert arrays or JSON-stringified arrays to CSV, and task.timeoutMs may convert numeric strings. New fields do not inherit magical fallback behavior; if a field needs compatibility, add an explicit normalization rule so future maintainers can see and audit it.
drafts.sourceHeading: 类型转换限定在已知字段

drafts.title: Unwrap stringified input twice
drafts.compiledTruth: Stringified dispatch input should be JSON-unwrapped at most two levels. One level covers a single mistaken stringify at the RPC boundary or prompt template; two levels covers the observed double-stringify case. Three or more levels is treated as caller error and left for schema validation to reject, preventing unbounded or over-permissive normalization.
drafts.sourceHeading: 双重 unwrap 逻辑上限

drafts.title: Compat errors teach repair
drafts.compiledTruth: Dispatch compatibility errors should be written for the next LLM retry. A good error includes the field name, expected type, actual received value plus unwrap progress, and a concrete corrected example. The error formatter should be shared by registered dispatch paths so model-facing repair hints stay consistent.

drafts.title: Subagents cannot dispatch subagents
drafts.compiledTruth: Subagent tool allowlists must always reject nested dispatch_agent or dispatch_parallel, regardless of requested tools or environment flags. Dispatch is a capability boundary controlled by the main session; allowing delegated agents to spawn more agents would blur accountability, amplify costs, and make orchestration hard to inspect.

drafts.title: Mutating tools need env gate
drafts.compiledTruth: Subagents default to read-only/search/brain-read style tools, not write authority. Mutating tools such as bash/edit/write must be rejected unless an explicit PI_MULTI_AGENT_ALLOW_MUTATING=1 environment gate allows them into the allowlist. Rejection should be explicit rather than silently dropping requested tools, so the main session can understand the failed delegation.

drafts.title: Vision and imagine are separate
drafts.compiledTruth: Vision and imagine are no longer bundled as part of the dispatch extension; they live as separate registered extensions. Dispatch semantics still allow a subagent to use those capabilities only when the main session explicitly lists them in the tools field, preserving delegation control without coupling image tools to multi-agent orchestration.

drafts.title: Dispatch parallelism is real IO concurrency
drafts.compiledTruth: Dispatch parallelism relies on Promise.all fanout over independent SDK LLM calls. Because calls are network-bound, Node async IO can keep tasks in flight concurrently and remote providers compute independently. The practical acceptance signal is wall time near the slowest task, not the sum of all task durations; concurrency regressions belong in tests rather than ADR-bound provider internals.
drafts.sourceHeading: 并发性事实(事实层,非决策)

drafts.title: Concurrency shares runtime limits
drafts.compiledTruth: Dispatch is true concurrent IO, but not process isolation. Tasks share the Node event loop, process memory, and normally a parent abort signal; CPU-heavy parsing or image work can briefly delay other tasks, uncaught task errors must be caught at the runner boundary, and a main-session cancel can cancel all children. These limits matter when designing long-lived background work or tool-heavy subagents.

drafts.title: Diversify providers for fanout
drafts.compiledTruth: Parallel model fanout should prefer cross-provider diversity when the tasks are independent reviewers or voters. Provider rate limits are often the real bottleneck for concurrent LLM calls; sending all branches to the same provider couples them to one quota, while spreading across providers reduces shared 429 risk and increases failure-mode diversity.

drafts.title: Test dispatch start skew
drafts.compiledTruth: A cheap regression test for dispatch concurrency is to inspect task start timestamps for a multi-task run. If three task starts are within about one second, fanout is happening concurrently; if starts serialize, dispatch has regressed into unusable behavior for latency-sensitive multi-model workflows. Keep this as a low-cost check after SDK or dispatch upgrades.
drafts.sourceHeading: 上线前验收(进 P3.x 验收标准)
